title: Media, Connectors, Transceivers, and Architectures · Guided Evidence Lab
summary: Select and document a campus and data-center physical design under distance, bandwidth, environment, and redundancy constraints. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a physical media and topology bill of design while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare 802.3 copper categories, shielding, distance, speed, duplex, PoE, coaxial, DAC, and twinax use cases.
Compare single-mode and multimode fiber, wavelengths, distance, bandwidth, bend, contamination, and safety concerns.
Compare 802.11, cellular, and satellite media by coverage, latency, capacity, interference, mobility, and cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
Use a changed workshop case derived from this module: network media topologies. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed workshop case derived from this module: network media topologies. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare 802.3 copper categories, shielding, distance, speed, duplex, poe, coaxial, dac, and twinax use cases.

content.text: Compare 802.3 copper categories, shielding, distance, speed, duplex, PoE, coaxial, DAC, and twinax use cases. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network media topologies. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Ethernet Media, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a physical media and topology bill of design while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A higher cable category always increases link speed without device or distance constraints. This fails the Net Ethernet Media contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: CompTIA Network Plus N10-009 Exam Objectives (N10-009 v4.0) — Controls every certification domain, objective, acronym, tool, protocol, and performance-based scope decision.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare 802.3 copper categories, shielding, distance, speed, duplex, PoE, coaxial, DAC, and twinax use cases.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Net Campus Datacenter Architecture condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Campus Datacenter Architecture applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare three-tier, collapsed-core, and spine-leaf architectures plus north-south and east-west traffic.
